Madam Kama Marg, Martyr Rajguru Chowk,

Read: - 1. Public Works Department, Government Order No. LTS-2026, / Q. No.78 / Service-2, D. 27/05/2026 2. Public Works Department, Government Order No. LTS-2026, / P.No.100 / Service-2, D. 17/06/2026 3. Public Works Department, Government Order No. LTS-2026 / P.No.100 / Service-2... (1), D 17/06/2026

The following engineer of the Deputy Engineer (Construction) cadre, under the relevant Government Orders, is posted at Column No. 3 The post / post mentioned herein was transferred / posted. However, with the approval of the competent authority under the provisions of the Maharashtra Regulation of Transfer of Government Employees Act, 2005, their revised postings are as shown below in column No. 4 is being done in the post here.
2. The aforesaid AIT engineers appointed under the Regional Department are informed that they should join immediately at the place of new appointment.

para_trans: Government Order No: LTS-2026 / P.No.100 / Service-2, D. 30/06/2026

AQ, the officers whose services have been acquired for the Special In-depth Review Programme of Electoral Lists-2026 out of the above table should not be relieved till the completion of this duty for joining the post of transfer / posting. Also, it should be ensured that the instructions of the Election Commission are not disregarded in any way.

Also, engineers in the said table will be liable for disciplinary action if they do not comply with this order. Also, the concerned Chief Engineer, Public Works Regional Department is informed that the date on which the engineers appointed under the said order in the Regional Department under his jurisdiction appeared at the place of the new appointment and if the said engineer does not appear immediately, the same should be immediately communicated to the Government by email at doser2.pwd @ maharashtra.gov.in. So that further action can be taken at the government level.

3. In the event of non-appearance at the place of appointment immediately in accordance with the order of such posting or any attempt to cancel the posting under any kind of pressure, the concerned shall be liable to disciplinary action on the ground that the act violates Rule 23 of the Maharashtra Civil Services (Conduct) Rules, 1979, treating it as misconduct.
The Government Order was made available on the Government of Maharashtra website www.maharashtra.gov.in.
